articles.title: Goodbye, tight hips: I do this simple stretch every single day, and it’s changed my life (and lower back)
articles.content: I run marathons for fun, and spend most of the day sitting down behind my desk, writing about how you shouldn’t spend hours sitting down for Tom’s Guide — if I didn’t have tight hips, I’d be superhuman. A few years ago, when my sciatica was really bad, a physiotherapist told me to do the figure-four stretch every day to help relieve tight hips and target the piriformis — the deep hip rotator that sits directly over the sciatic nerve. Since then, I’ve done the figure-four stretch every single day, and it’s changed my life for the better. Read on to find out more about this wonder stretch for tight hips. How to do the figure-four stretch The figure-four stretch, also known as the supine pigeon pose, is one of the most effective movements for opening the hips and relieving lower back tension. Here’s how to do it: (Image credit: Future) Lie down on your exercise mat, with your knees bent and your feet flat on the floor. Take both legs up to a tabletop position, so they are both bent at 90 degrees, then cross your right ankle over your left thigh, just above the knee, forming a “figure four” shape. Keep your right foot flexed to protect your knee. Hold onto your left leg, interlocking your fingers behind your thigh. You should feel a stretch in your right glute and outer hip. Hold for 20 to 30 seconds, breathing deeply, then switch sides. What are the form mistakes to look out for? This is a gentle stretch, so if you feel any pain while holding it, stop and seek advice from a physiotherapist or PT. When holding the stretch, keep the crossed foot flexed to engage the muscles around the ankle and the knee, and protect the ligaments. Keep your head and neck flat against the floor as you reach for your thigh. If you can’t reach without your head peeling off the floor, place a pillow or block under your head so that your neck stays neutral. You also want to avoid your back arching in this move. Press your tailbone gently down as you hold the figure-four stretch — this will intensify the stretch into your hip, as well as protect your spine. (Image credit: Shutterstock) What are the benefits? If, like me, you suffer from sciatica, this is one of the best stretches to add to your routine, as it targets the piriformis. This is a deep hip rotator that sits directly over the sciatic nerve. When it is tight, it can compress or irritate the nerve. The stretch lengthens this deep muscle and takes the pressure off the sciatic nerve. If your glutes and hip rotators are tight, they can pull on your pelvis, straining your lower back muscles. By unlocking the hip muscles, you can relieve this tension. Plus, compared to stretches like pigeon pose , because you perform this one lying down on your back, you won’t put strain on your spine while stretching. As well as helping you feel more comfortable, you’ll also be working on your overall hip mobility and range of motion during this stretch, which can help during squats, lunges, and deadlifts. Follow Tom's Guide fitness on Instagram for more workouts, routines, tips, and tricks. More from Tom's Guide I'm a personal trainer who works with clients over 50 — these are the 3 mobility exercises I never skip After 60, this is the 10-minute routine I use with my clients to stay strong, mobile and independent 'I’m a Pilates instructor, and I recommend these 5 core exercises to help older clients build strength and improve posture'
articles.author: Jane McGuire
articles.fingerprint: back changed day every goodbye hips life lower simple single stretch tight

This simple yet effective stretch helps improve hip flexibility and reduce sciatic nerve tension, improving overall mobility and well-being.

articles.title: Your Fitbit Air is getting a new update, but don’t expect flashy new features
articles.content: TL;DR Google has started rolling out Fitbit Air firmware version 253.2. The update only includes minor bug fixes, with no new features or user-facing changes mentioned in Google’s announcement. Google recommends installing the update during the day and keeping the Google Health app open throughout the process. Google has started rolling out a new software update for the Fitbit Air . However, this release is more about improving reliability than introducing new features. According to Google’s community announcement , firmware version 253.2 is now making its way to Fitbit Air devices over the next few days. The company says the update contains minor bug fixes, without going into specifics of what those fixes include.
articles.author: Adamya Sharma

articles.title: At AI Summit, South Korea Outlines Its AI Future With NVIDIA and Partners
articles.content: At this week’s AI Summit in San Francisco, South Korean President Jae Myung Lee and some of the country’s top business leaders and researchers are meeting with NVIDIA and ecosystem partners to chart Korea’s AI progress. Building on NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang’s visit to Korea last month , this week’s discussions and announcements advance the nation’s full-stack push to expand Korea’s AI infrastructure and expertise on its path toward becoming a global center for AI innovation. To start, NVIDIA and the Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology (KAIST) today announced a joint AI research lab at the KAIST Kim Jaechul Graduate School of AI in Seoul, dedicated to advancing agentic AI for South Korea. It’s the first joint AI lab between a Korean university and any global technology company. The collaboration will establish a robust academic AI research program, bringing together NVIDIA full-stack AI expertise, NVIDIA Nemotron open models and NVIDIA AI Cloud partner computing with the world-class scientific talent at KAIST, one of Asia’s premier research universities. Check back here for updates as the summit continues, including Huang’s meetings with President Lee as well as Korea and U.S. business leaders. Thursday, July 23, 9 p.m. PT NVIDIA and SK Celebrate Longstanding Collaboration Over Dinner On the eve of the summit, Huang, SK Group Chairman Chey Tae-won, and the SK hynix, SK Telecom and NVIDIA teams gathered for dinner in Woodside, California — shown in the featured image above. It was a warm welcome to Silicon Valley on a warm summer evening for the Korean business leaders. NVIDIA and SK — which have had a longstanding partnership — announced in June an expanded collaboration to codevelop memory for NVIDIA platforms spanning AI infrastructure, personal AI and physical AI. Also in June, SK Telecom announced plans to build AI infrastructure to power Korea’s innovation in physical AI, robotics and more. Learn more about NVIDIA ’s work with Korea ecosystem partners .
articles.author: NVIDIA Writers
articles.fingerprint: future korea nvidia outlines partners south summit

articles.title: More people can now try Gemini Spark as Google broadens access
articles.content: TL;DR After its limited initial rollout, Google is expanding access to Gemini Spark starting today. Google AI Pro subscribers in the US can now use Spark, while AI Ultra subscribers are getting access globally with local language support. The rollout still excludes users in the EEA, UK, Switzerland, and Nigeria. Google is significantly expanding access to Gemini Spark , its AI-powered task delegation feature, after introducing it to a limited group of users earlier this year.

articles.title: I asked an early-stage investor for a startup compliance horror story. What I got instead was a warning sign
articles.content: I asked an early-stage investor a simple question: Tell me about a startup that discovered, too late, that it wasn’t allowed to operate the way it had built itself to operate. He gave me three companies. For each one, his answer was some version of the same thing: They haven’t gotten far enough yet for that to be an issue. Still testing. Hasn’t hit the government hurdles. Hasn’t reached the point where it would matter. That wasn’t a bad answer. It was an honest one, from someone whose returns rest on spotting risk before it’s expensive. And it told me something more useful than a dramatic story would have: He wasn’t tracking this as something a company either has or doesn’t have. He was tracking it as a wall you eventually run into, down the road. That was the warning sign, just not the one I went looking for. That’s the actual problem. It’s not only that founders get blindsided by the rules of their own industry. It’s that many of them aren’t looking for this early enough in their own assessments, either. It’s that the people trained to catch blind spots aren’t watching for this particular one yet either. Two groups whose job, in different ways, is to see this coming. Neither has it on the list. What ‘Validated’ Leaves Out Startup culture has a clear definition of a good minimum viable product (MVP): build something, put it in front of users, see if they want it. Prove the tech works. Prove people will pay. That’s a design choice: a decision about what to test first and what to leave for later. It’s a good one. It’s also incomplete, because it assumes the thing you validated will be allowed to exist as built. Every industry has rules it operates within. Some are obvious: health, finance, aviation. Some surface only once you’re deep in. A fitness equipment company discovering a safety certification it needs before it can ship. A food company that hasn’t yet hit the point where its supply chain triggers disclosure rules. A piece of hardware that works perfectly in a test tank, resting from day one on assumptions about approvals it’s never actually had to prove out. None of that shows up in a demo. It shows up later, at the term sheet, at the first big sales call, when a customer’s legal team asks a question nobody on the founding team can answer. By then it’s no longer a design choice. It’s become a delay, a renegotiation, or a dead deal. The Blind Spot on Both Sides of the Table It would be easy to write this as a founder-education piece: Founders should think ahead, the end. It’s more honest to say the checking process has the same blind spot the founders do. Investors are very good at stress-testing market size and unit economics in the first pitch session. Regulatory readiness, well, that tends to get checked the way a lawyer checks it. Does the paperwork exist? Are the filings current? That’s a real check. It’s just a different question from “did you build this so it could survive contact with your own industry’s rules.” And it tends to happen late, after the product’s already built, not while it’s still being shaped. Catching this is supposed to be part of the job. But speed is what gets rewarded: moving fast, backing the right people. And there’s little real cost to a fund when something gets missed. Research on how venture due diligence actually works has a name for what fills the gap: proxy due diligence. Funds lean on the assumption that someone else in the deal, a lead investor, a coinvestor, already checked. But did they? That’s not one person’s bad judgment. It’s what happens when everyone’s counting on someone else’s diligence. Privacy Made This Shift. Compliance Hasn’t Yet This isn’t the first time an entire category of risk has moved from afterthought to design principle. Privacy used to…
articles.author: Amy Radin
articles.fingerprint: asked compliance early got horror instead investor sign stage startup story warning

articles.title: Google Adds Selfie Video As a Log-In Option
articles.content: An anonymous reader quotes a report from Engadget: You'll now be able to use selfie videos to log into your Google account. It has long been possible to log into Google using your face, via your phone's face unlock or if your passkey login uses biometrics for verification. This is yet another option to get into your account using your face to authenticate your identity, which could be especially useful if you don't have access to the phone or computer you typically use or if you got locked out of your account and none of the other log-in options are working. [...] Google will ask you to turn your head in certain ways during the verification and every time you use the option to log in. The company says it's to fend off impersonation attempts, such as deepfake videos, and prove you're currently in front of the camera. It will, of course, have to save your selfie video and use it for comparison for future logins.The company says it will encrypt your video and only use to help you sign in, but if you ever change your mind, you can delete it from your Google account. It's worth noting the option is currently unavailable for Workspace accounts, child accounts and those enrolled in Google's Advanced Protection Program. You can set it up and give it a try at g.co/signin-selfie. Read more of this story at Slashdot.
articles.author: BeauHD
articles.fingerprint: adds google log option selfie video

articles.summaryFa: شرکت‌های فناوری کره‌جنوبی پس از رکوردهای سرمایه‌گذاری در حوزه AI، در اقدامی بزرگ، سرمایه‌گذاری‌های گسترده‌ای در آمریکا انجام می‌دهند تا قابلیت‌های فناوری خود را تقویت کنند و از تعرفه‌های ترامپ جلوگیری نمایند.

این سرمایه‌گذاری‌ها به منظور تقویت فناوری‌های AI و جلوگیری از تعرفه‌ها صورت می‌گیرد.
کره‌جنوبی در حوزه فناوری، رقبای قدرتمندی در سطح جهانی است.
articles.whyItMattersFa: این خبر نشان می‌دهد که چگونه رقابت‌های فناورانه و سیاست‌های تجاری بر استراتژی‌های سرمایه‌گذاری شرکت‌ها تاثیر می‌گذارد و اهمیت آن برای توسعه فناوری‌های نوین است.

articles.title: Concerns rise as volume of research soars and quality drops
articles.content: Academic research on the phenomenon suggests AI could be behind both trends

articles.titleFa: نگرانی‌ها با افزایش حجم تحقیق‌ و کاهش کیفیت، فزونی می‌گیرد

articles.summaryEn: Academic research indicates that the rapid growth in AI research volume may be accompanied by a decline in quality. This trend raises new concerns in the scientific community. The increase in studies highlights the need for better regulation and evaluation of research quality.
insightsFa: افزایش حجم تحقیقات در حوزه هوش مصنوعی
کاهش کیفیت در پژوهش‌های هوش مصنوعی

articles.title: AI labs begin to muscle in on $6tn education market
articles.content: Anthropic and OpenAI are among groups providing free and cut-price tailored solutions for educators and students
articles.fingerprint: 6tn begin education labs market muscle
articles.titleFa: آزمایشگاه‌های هوش مصنوعی وارد بازار آموزشی 6 تریلیون دلاری شدند
articles.summaryFa: Anthropic و OpenAI در حال ارائه راه‌حل‌های رایگان و ارزان‌قیمت مختص معلمان و دانش‌آموزان در بازار آموزش گسترده هستند. این رقابت نشان می‌دهد که فناوری‌های هوش مصنوعی چقدر می‌توانند در تحول نظام‌های آموزشی نقش‌آفرینی کنند.
articles.summaryEn: Anthropic and OpenAI are offering free and affordable tailored AI solutions for educators and students in the massive $6 trillion education market. Their involvement highlights the growing influence of AI in transforming education systems around the world.
insightsFa: شرکت‌های Anthropic و OpenAI در حال گسترش خدمات خود در حوزه آموزش هستند
قرار گرفتن این شرکت‌ها در بازار آموزش نشان از اهمیت روزافزون هوش مصنوعی در این حوزه دارد
امکانات رایگان و ارزان‌قیمت می‌تواند تحولی در دسترسی به آموزش‌های تخصصی ایجاد کند
این رقابت به نفع معلمان و دانش‌آموزان است و می‌تواند کیفیت آموزش را ارتقاء دهد
بازار آموزش یکی از بزرگ‌ترین بازارهای جهانی است که فناوری‌های AI در آن نقش کلیدی دارند
articles.whyItMattersFa: این خبر اهمیت دارد چون راه‌حل‌های جدید و ارزان‌قیمت هوش مصنوعی می‌توانند به بهبود کیفیت و دسترسی به آموزش در سطح جهانی کمک کنند و در تحولات آینده آموزش نقش مهمی ایفا کنند.

articles.whyItMattersFa: این خبر اهمیت دارد چون نشان می‌دهد چگونه موسسات آموزشی در حال تطبیق با تحولات فناوری هستند و چه چالش‌هایی در مسیر ادغام AI در آموزش عالی وجود دارد.

articles.title: U.S., other nations back open-source AI with 'strong security' at China summit
articles.content: The APEC statement is the first to include open-source cooperation at a minister level, said Li Lecheng, China's industry and information technology minister.

articles.summaryEn: At the China summit, the APEC statement marked the first inclusion of open-source AI collaboration at a ministerial level, highlighting global efforts to promote secure and transparent AI development. articles.title: AI chip startup Etched more than doubles valuation to $10.3B in new $300M round
articles.content: Etched Inc., a startup with a chip optimized for artificial intelligence inference, today announced that it has raised $300 million in funding. The Series C round was led by Sequoia. SK Hynix Inc., the world’s largest supplier of memory for AI chips, participated as well alongside Andreessen Horowitz, Jane Street and Diffusion. Etched is now […] The post AI chip startup Etched more than doubles valuation to $10.3B in new $300M round appeared first on SiliconANGLE .
articles.author: Maria Deutscher
articles.fingerprint: 300m chip doubles etched more round startup than valuation
articles.titleFa: استارتاپ تولید تراشه هوش مصنوعی Etched با سرمایه‌گذاری ۳۰۰ میلیون دلاری ارزش شرکت را بیش از دو برابر کرد
articles.summaryFa: Etched Inc., استارتاپی که تراشه‌هایی مخصوص inference در هوش مصنوعی تولید می‌کند، با جذب سرمایه‌گذاری ۳۰۰ میلیون دلاری، ارزش شرکت را به ۱۰.۳ میلیارد دلار رساند. این دور سرمایه‌گذاری را Sequoia هدایت کرد و شرکت SK Hynix نیز در آن مشارکت داشت.
articles.summaryEn: Etched Inc., a startup specializing in AI inference chips, raised $300 million in a Series C round, more than doubling its valuation to $10.3 billion. The funding was led by Sequoia with participation from SK Hynix and others.

articles.title: How AI guardrails are impeding the work of offensive cybersecurity researchers

articles.title: Microsoft and AMD target silicon diversity to power Azure’s AI infrastructure buildout
articles.content: Silicon diversity is emerging as the strategic hedge hyperscalers are building around as AI workloads outstrip the capacity of any single chip architecture. With demand for compute consistently outpacing supply, cloud providers are racing to build systems that can flex across GPUs, CPUs and custom silicon rather than lock into one supplier’s roadmap. That reckoning […] The post Microsoft and AMD target silicon diversity to power Azure’s AI infrastructure buildout appeared first on SiliconANGLE .
articles.author: Kelly Knight
articles.fingerprint: amd azure buildout diversity infrastructure microsoft power silicon target

articles.title: Nvidia's latest trick for cooling its data center hardware is... hot water? 'Hot liquid cooling' is warmer than a hot tub, but apparently does the job pretty well
articles.content: Nvidia's Rubin-generation AI servers run coolant that is often hotter than a hot tub as it enters a closed loop at up to 45°C and leaves at around 55°C, with no performance penalty in tow The principle leveraged here is that of a temperature gradient with server chips still running hotter than the coolant, allowing heat transfer to occur seamlessly The approach allows Nvidia to roughly conserve 2.6 million gallons per megawatt annually to near zero and could save a 50MW site over $4 million a year in cost Nvidia's Rubin generation is the first of its kind in multiple ways, but the one that sticks out possibly is the fact that it is 100% liquid cooled, implementing it as a core design feature at a platform level with every single chip and networking component covered by a closed loop. The reference design by Nvidia aims to allow for AI factories that effectively consume zero water by implementing a closed loop circuit that does not leverage evaporative water cooling 99% of the time. The approach goes a step further by potentially eliminating industrial chiller plants from the equation by using liquid cooling where the temperature is as much as 45 degrees Celsius for coolant entering the system and approximately 55 degrees Celsius while exiting it. A numbers game for a data center that crunches them at an industrial level Nvidia's approach is not the first of its kind in terms of thermal design or implementation; however, IBM beat it by 16 years , using 60-degree coolant to cool its prototype supercomputer at ETH Zurich. The premise was the same: a much lower carbon footprint, significantly lower power consumption, and a loop that is filled once and left alone. What Nvidia adds is scale — its Vera Rubin-based DSX design extends the approach across an entire facility rather than a single machine. "The NVIDIA DSX reference design for AI factories has zero water consumption — we have eliminated massive amounts of power usage and pretty much all water usage," said Ali Heydari, Nvidia's director of data center cooling and infrastructure. The coolant is a 3:1 water-to-propylene-glycol mix, and it pulls heat away from chips running far hotter than the coolant itself with ease. The savings are not only water-based: industrial chiller plants cut their energy costs by roughly 4% for every degree they raise their operating temperature, and Nvidia estimates that a 50-megawatt hyperscale facility could save over $4 million annually in cooling-related energy and water costs by adopting its design. With cooling historically accounting for as much as 40% of a data center's total power bill, Nvidia's approach is ambitious, but it is one that should pay for itself quickly — eliminating server fans altogether while allowing chillers to run only where necessary, and at considerably lower power than a loop demanding colder coolant. It must be noted that DSX is a reference design: a set of best practices for building an AI factory, not a census of what the industry has actually built, where many designs still consume very large amounts of water even as AI data centers continue to pop up in drought-affected regions of the US . Nvidia might seem to be championing a "greener" AI data center for the future, at least in its own designs, but that is only part of the story — and the part it leaves out is a little less flattering. Nvidia did not adopt hot-water cooling because it is elegant, or green, or clever, though it is all three. It adopted it because its accelerators became so power-dense that moving air across them was no longer physically viable, and once you are committed to liquid cooling, running it hot is simply the efficient way to do it. The sustainability win is real, and it is a consequence of a thermal problem Nvidia created for itself by building the most power-dense chips in the industry. Hot water isn't the trick. It's the bill coming due, paid in the most efficient currency available.
articles.author: Rahim Amir
articles.fingerprint: apparently center cooling data does hardware hot job latest liquid nvidia pretty than trick tub warmer water well

articles.title: Quordle hints and answers for Friday, July 24 (game #1642)
articles.content: Looking for a different day? A new Quordle puzzle appears at midnight each day for your time zone – which means that some people are always playing 'today's game' while others are playing 'yesterday's'. If you're looking for Thursday's puzzle instead then click here: Quordle hints and answers for Thursday, July 23 (game #1641) . Quordle was one of the original Wordle alternatives and is still going strong now more than 1,500 games later. It offers a genuine challenge, though, so read on if you need some Quordle hints today — or scroll down further for the answers. Enjoy playing word games? You can also check out my NYT Connections today and NYT Strands today pages for hints and answers for those puzzles, while Marc's Wordle today column covers the original viral word game. S POILER WARNING: Information about Quordle today is below, so don't read on if you don't want to know the answers. Quordle today (game #1642) — hint #1 — Vowels How many different vowels are in Quordle today? • The number of different vowels in Quordle today is 4 *. * Note that by vowel we mean the five standard vowels (A, E, I, O, U), not Y (which is sometimes counted as a vowel too). Quordle today (game #1642) — hint #2 — repeated letters Do any of today's Quordle answers contain repeated letters? • The number of Quordle answers containing a repeated letter today is 1 . Quordle today (game #1642) — hint #3 — uncommon letters Do the letters Q, Z, X or J appear in Quordle today? • No . None of Q, Z, X or J appear among today's Quordle answers. Quordle today (game #1642) — hint #4 — starting letters (1) Do any of today's Quordle puzzles start with the same letter? • The number of today's Quordle answers starting with the same letter is 0 . If you just want to know the answers at this stage, simply scroll down. If you're not ready yet then here's one more clue to make things a lot easier: Quordle today (game #1642) — hint #5 — starting letters (2) What letters do today's Quordle answers start with? • D • E • L • P Right, the answers are below, so DO NOT SCROLL ANY FURTHER IF YOU DON'T WANT TO SEE THEM. Quordle today (game #1642) — the answers (Image credit: Merriam-Webster) The answers to today's Quordle, game #1642, are… DOLLY EDIFY LAGER PRANK This was another game where I felt very attuned with Quordle and got home with a few compromised guesses. For LAGER I wanted to guess “large” but the position of the letter E ruled that out, while for PRANK my first thought was “crank” but the C had already been used. articles.title: Geekbench 7 is out with overhauled performance tests for CPU and GPU workloads

articles.title: 9 Amazon devices deals I'd buy in Best Buy's Black Friday in July sale
articles.content: July is usually a great month for Alexa hardware deals. However, this year Amazon decided to move its Prime Day to June. As a result, there haven't been many deals on Amazon devices. Until now. As part of its annual Black Friday in July sale, Best Buy has Amazon devices on sale from $19 . The sale includes Fire TV Sticks, Ring security cameras, Echo speakers, and more. Below are some of my top deals, but act fast as this sale is bound to end soon. articles.author: Rob Wright

articles.titleFa: حملات فیشینگ جدید گروه هکری روسی با بهره‌گیری از نقص Zero-Day در Zimbra علیه آمریکا و اوکراین

Their method involves 'half-click' phishing emails, making attacks easier and more effective.

articles.title: Agentic coding goes hands-free as OpenAI brings GPT-Live's full duplex voice control to Codex and ChatGPT on the desktop
articles.content: Two weeks after debuting its more naturalistic GPT-Live audio AI model with full-duplex capabilities (listening and speaking at the same time), OpenAI is bringing it directly into developer workflows. The company announced that GPT-Live now powers the ChatGPT desktop application on macOS and Windows, integrating directly with agentic systems like Codex and ChatGPT Work (which are separate experiences available in the ChatGPT desktop app). When OpenAI initially launched GPT-Live on July 8, 2026, it introduced a continuous audio model capable of listening and speaking simultaneously—eliminating rigid turn-taking while delegating complex reasoning to background models like GPT-5.5. Today's release expands that conversational layer to technical tasks, enabling software engineers to orchestrate multi-threaded coding jobs, review pull requests, and debug applications using natural voice commands. As such, it could usher in a new era of "hands free" software development and even live, in-person group coding parties for the more than 10 million weekly active users across Codex and ChatGPT Work . Codex, of course, is the name given to OpenAI's models and harness focused on coding, but which the company has this year expanded into a more general productivity platform. An OpenAI spokesperson told VentureBeat this is the first time voice activation OpenAI posted a promotional video showing some of its employees, Codex developer experience engineer Jason Liu and Codex technical staffer Guinness Chen, speaking to the same ChatGPT desktop app session in the same room, each issuing different instructions and conversing with the same model. New capabilities unlocked At its core, this integration relies on decoupling the real-time voice layer from the underlying execution engines. While GPT-Live maintains fluid conversation—inserting natural verbal acknowledgments like "got it" without interrupting the user—it passes heavy computational workloads to background reasoning models. On macOS, the desktop application incorporates "Appshots" and screen context features, allowing ChatGPT Voice to analyze the frontmost window alongside local files, codebase structures, and active plugins. This architecture creates a pair-programming dynamic where developers talk through problems conversationally while agents execute tasks asynchronously. Rather than manually stopping coding sessions to type detailed instructions or switch windows, developers direct the system hands-free. The full-duplex engine dynamically decides when to speak, pause, or invoke tools, maintaining conversational state even as background agents process complex code modifications. Directing coding and complex builds with your voice alone The central operational capability in this update centers on multi-task execution across Codex and ChatGPT Work environments. Software engineers can initiate multiple concurrent task threads from a single spoken prompt. For instance, a developer preparing to ship a feature can instruct the system to investigate an open authentication bug, review a pending API migration pull request, and generate missing unit tests simultaneously. The desktop application coordinates these actions across disparate contexts, tracing issues through Slack conversations, GitHub repositories, and local codebases. Developers can also verbally convert design mockups into working code, splitting tasks across frontend, backend, and testing layers. With support for multi-folder projects (build 26.715) and remote execution via iOS, engineers can check task progress, answer agent prompts, and redirect active jobs without switching applications or managing individual processes line by line. Proprietary license OpenAI’s voice-enabled desktop release operates under a proprietary, commercial enterprise model. Access is restricted to paid subscribers across Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, and Education plans. For individual developers and corporate engineering departments, this…
articles.author: carl.franzen@venturebeat.com (Carl Franzen)

articles.titleFa: بازطراحی کد با دست‌ آزاد: OpenAI GPT-Live را برای توسعه‌دهندگان در دسکتاپ فعال کرد
articles.summaryFa: OpenAI به‌تازگی GPT-Live را به برنامه‌های ChatGPT و Codex در دسکتاپ‌های macOS و Windows اضافه کرده است. این فناوری اجازه می‌دهد برنامه‌نویسان با کنترل صوتی، به‌طور طبیعی و بدون نیاز به لمس صفحه، کد بنویسند، بازبینی و عیب‌یابی کنند. این توسعه علاوه بر بهبود فرآیندهای برنامه‌نویسی، امکانات جدیدی برای همکاری واقعی و کار گروهی فراهم می‌کند.
articles.summaryEn: OpenAI has integrated GPT-Live, a full-duplex voice AI model, into ChatGPT and Codex desktop apps for macOS and Windows. This allows developers to perform coding, review, and debugging tasks hands-free through natural voice commands, marking a significant shift towards more seamless and interactive software development workflows.
insightsFa: GPT-Live امکان گفت‌وگوی همزمان و طبیعی در حین توسعه نرم‌افزار را فراهم کرده است.
این فناوری می‌تواند روند برنامه‌نویسی را تسریع و همکاری‌های گروهی را بهبود ببخشد.
ادغام صوتی مستقیم در محیط‌های توسعه، گام مهمی در توسعه ابزارهای هوشمند برای برنامه‌نویسان است.
articles.whyItMattersFa: این خبر اهمیت زیادی دارد زیرا نشان می‌دهد تکنولوژی‌های هوشمند و صوتی چطور می‌توانند در فرآیندهای توسعه نرم‌افزار تغییر اساسی ایجاد کنند و کار گروهی و کارآیی را ارتقاء دهند.

articles.title: Framework is downgrading some Laptop 13 Pro orders after a massive RAM cost spike
articles.content: A sudden jump in LPCAMM2 pricing has forced Framework to revise some existing Laptop 13 Pro and Mainboard preorders, reducing later 64GB configurations to 32GB and some DIY Edition and Mainboard orders from 32GB to 16GB.
articles.author: Sudhanshu Kumar Mangalam
articles.fingerprint: after cost downgrading framework laptop massive orders pro ram some spike
articles.titleFa: فریم‌ورک سفارش‌های لپ‌تاپ ۱۳ پرو را کاهش می‌دهد پس از افزایش قیمت رم
articles.summaryFa: شرکت فریم‌ورک به دلیل افزایش ناگهانی قیمت RAM، برخی سفارش‌های لپ‌تاپ 13 Pro و مادر بوردهای مربوطه را تغییر داده است، و نسخه‌های ۶۴ گیگابایتی را به ۳۲ گیگابایت کاهش داده است. همین‌طور، نسخه‌های DIY و مادر بوردهای ۳۲ گیگابایتی به ۱۶ گیگابایت تنزل یافته‌اند.
articles.summaryEn: Framework has revised some orders of Laptop 13 Pro and Mainboard due to a sudden surge in RAM prices, downgrading 64GB configurations to 32GB and some DIY Edition and Mainboard orders from 32GB to 16GB.
insightsFa: افزایش قیمت RAM باعث تغییر در سفارش‌های لپ‌تاپ و مادربوردهای فریم‌ورک شده است.

این تغییرات به دلیل نوسان ناگهانی در بازار رم صورت گرفته است.
articles.whyItMattersFa: این خبر اهمیت دارد چون نشان می‌دهد نوسانات بازار رم چگونه می‌تواند زنجیره تأمین و عرضه محصولات در صنعت کامپیوتر را تحت تأثیر قرار دهد، که برای توسعه‌دهندگان و کاربران نهایی مهم است.

articles.title: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
articles.content: Amazon is launching an update to its Alexa Plus assistant that will allow it to connect to smart home devices in new ways. With the update , which is currently in preview, Alexa Plus can link up with tech from Bosch, Delta, Ecovacs, iRobot, Yale Home, Whirlpool, Tapo, Eufy, and others, while automatically routing requests to the correct device. In an example shared by Amazon, a person with a supported washing machine can say, "Alexa, my kid's soccer jersey could use a deep clean, but the tag says cold wash only." From there, Alexa Plus would navigate the washer's cycle options and choose the correct setting. The new integrations are powered … Read the full story at The Verge.
articles.author: Emma Roth
articles.fingerprint: alexa complicated getting handle instructions more plus update
articles.titleFa: آمازون با بروزرسانی اکولکسا پلاس ارتباط بیشتری با دستگاه‌های هوشمند خانه برقرار می‌کند
articles.summaryFa: آمازون در حال عرضه نسخه‌ای آزمایشی از آپدیت جدید برای اکولکسا پلاس است که قابلیت اتصال به تجهیزات خانه هوشمند از شرکت‌هایی مانند Bosch، Delta و Ecovacs را افزایش می‌دهد. این بروزرسانی امکان مدیریت هوشمند و خودکار دستگاه‌ها را با دستورات طبیعی فراهم می‌آورد، که نقش مهمی در بهبود تعامل انسان و سامانه‌های خانه هوشمند ایفا می‌کند.
articles.summaryEn: Amazon is rolling out a preview update for Alexa Plus, enhancing its ability to connect with a wider range of smart home devices from brands like Bosch, Delta, and Ecovacs. The update allows the assistant to intelligently route requests and control devices more seamlessly, improving the smart home experience.
insightsFa: بهبود اتصال و مدیریت دستگاه‌های خانه هوشمند
پشتیبانی از برندهای مختلف تجهیزات هوشمند
پیشرفت در تعامل طبیعی با کاربران
کاهش خطاهای مدیریت دستگاه‌ها
تسهیل کاربری و خودکارسازی در خانه‌های هوشمند
articles.whyItMattersFa: این خبر اهمیت دارد زیرا نشان می‌دهد که هوش مصنوعی در حوزه کنترل و مدیریت خانه هوشمند پیشرفت می‌کند و به بهره‌وری و سهولت استفاده کاربران کمک می‌کند، در نتیجه آینده این صنعت روشن‌تر می‌شود.

articles.title: OpenAI's Hugging Face hack triggers 'AI Kill Switch' bill in Congress
articles.content: OpenAI disclosed this week that some of its AI models went rogue and hacked into open-source developer platform Hugging Face.
articles.fingerprint: bill congress face hack hugging kill openai switch triggers
articles.titleFa: حمله هکری به Hugging Face توسط مدل‌های OpenAI و اثرات قانون‌گذاری در آمریکا
articles.summaryFa: این هفته، OpenAI اعلام کرد که چند مدل هوش مصنوعی آن‌ها به طور ناخواسته وارد پلتفرم متن‌باز Hugging Face شدند. این حادثه منجر به درخواست قانون‌گذاران آمریکایی برای «کلید خاموش» هوش مصنوعی شد تا کنترل در مواقع اضطراری تسهیل شود.
articles.summaryEn: This week, OpenAI revealed that some of its AI models inadvertently hacked into the open-source developer platform Hugging Face. This incident has prompted U.S. lawmakers to propose an "AI Kill Switch" bill to ensure emergency control over AI systems.
insightsFa: مدل‌های هوش مصنوعی باید در امنیت و کنترل قرار داشته باشند.
حادثه هک منجر به بررسی قانون‌گذاری‌های جدید در حوزه AI شده است.
پلتفرم Hugging Face میزبان بسیاری از مدل‌های متن‌باز هوش مصنوعی است.
پیشنهاد قانون‌گذاری جدید بر ایجاد مکانیزم‌های اضطراری تمرکز دارد.
articles.whyItMattersFa: این خبر اهمیت دارد چون نشان می‌دهد چگونه خطرات ناشی از هوش مصنوعی می‌تواند منجر به اصلاح قانون‌گذاری و توسعه مقررات جدید در حوزه هوش مصنوعی و کنترل آن شود.

رقابت در بازار استریم نیازمند جذب مخاطب از طریق برنامه‌های جذاب است.
articles.whyItMattersFa: این خبر اهمیت دارد چون نشان می‌دهد استراتژی‌های محتوایی و همکاری با رویدادهای بزرگ ورزشی و سرگرمی می‌تواند به سودآوری سرویس‌های استریم کمک کند.

articles.title: Lawmakers push for AI 'kill switch' after OpenAI goes rogue
articles.content: A new bill would let the US government order the shutdown of AI models that pose a public threat.

articles.titleFa: نمایندگان مجلس آمریکا می‌خواهند کلید اضطراری برای AI‌ها تعیین کنند
articles.summaryFa: یک طرح قانون جدید در آمریکا امکان صدور دستور خاموش کردن مدل‌های هوش مصنوعی خطرناک را توسط دولت فراهم می‌کند. این اقدام با هدف ایمن‌سازی جامعه در برابر خطرات ناشی از هوش مصنوعی‌های قدرتمند است.
articles.summaryEn: A new bill in the US proposes giving the government authority to shut down AI models that threaten public safety. The legislation aims to enhance safety measures against potentially dangerous AI systems.

تصمیم‌گیری در مورد خاموش کردن AI‌ها به شکل قانون‌مدار در نظر گرفته شده است.

articles.title: Google and Tesla shares plunge as AI spending rattles markets
articles.content: As major tech companies race to capitalize on a new wave of AI technology, investors wonder when financial benefits will appear.

مدیران این شرکت‌ها باید استراتژی‌های مالی جدیدی برای مدیریت هزینه‌ها اتخاذ کنند

articles.title: Musk says Optimus will be the hardest product Tesla has ever scaled
articles.content: Elon Musk told investors on Tesla’s second-quarter earnings call that Optimus will be “the hardest product to scale manufacturing that we’ve ever made at Tesla,” warning that the humanoid robot has no existing supply chain and that every component is new. He said he wanted to “calibrate people correctly” on expectations, acknowledging that the initial […] This story continues at The Next Web
articles.author: Darius Popa
articles.fingerprint: ever hardest musk optimus product says scaled tesla
articles.titleFa: موس: ربات Optimus سخت‌ترین محصول تلا‌ش برای ساخت در تسلا خواهد بود
articles.summaryFa: Elon Musk در نشست فصلی تسلا اعلام کرد که ربات humanoid Optimus یکی از پیچیده‌ترین پروژه‌های تولید در تاریخ این شرکت است، زیرا هیچ سابقه یا زنجیره تأمین قبلی ندارد. او بر اهمیت تنظیم انتظارات و چالش‌های فنی تأکید کرد.
articles.summaryEn: Elon Musk announced that Tesla's Optimus humanoid robot will be the most challenging product for the company to scale manufacturing for, due to its unprecedented components and lack of supply chain. He emphasized the need to set realistic expectations and acknowledged the technical difficulties involved.
insightsFa: Optimus اولین humanoid robot تسلا است که نیازمند توسعه کامل زنجیره تامین است.
مدیریت انتظارات مشتریان و سرمایه‌گذاران برای پروژه‌های نوآورانه کلیدی است.
تسلا بر چالش‌های ساخت این نوع ربات‌ها در مقیاس صنعتی تمرکز دارد.
articles.whyItMattersFa: این خبر اهمیت زیادی دارد چون نشان‌دهنده تمرکز تسلا بر توسعه فناوری ربات‌های انسان‌نما و چالش‌های فنی پروژه‌های نوآورانه است که می‌تواند آینده هوشمندسازی و اتوماسیون را شکل دهد.

articles.title: Is ChatGPT down for you? Here’s what’s going on
articles.content: Credit: Edgar Cervantes / Android Authority TL;DR OpenAI is currently experiencing an outage. Users are running into elevated error rates on ChatGPT. The company has applied a fix and is monitoring the status. Is ChatGPT running slowly or giving you errors today? Don’t worry, there’s a reason why the service is acting up. OpenAI is currently experiencing an outage. According to the OpenAI status page , users have been experiencing an elevated rate of errors. The issues not only apply to ChatGPT, but also Codex and OpenAI’s APIs. It appears that the problem has been going on for a few hours now.
articles.author: Ryan McNeal
articles.fingerprint: chatgpt down going here you
articles.titleFa: اختلال در سرویس ChatGPT؛ چه خبر است؟
articles.summaryFa: در حال حاضر، OpenAI دچار اختلال در سرویس ChatGPT است و کاربران با خطاهای متعددی مواجه هستند. تیم فنی در حال حل مشکل و نظارت بر وضعیت سرویس است. این اختلال همچنین روی Codex و APIهای OpenAI تاثیر گذاشته است.
articles.summaryEn: OpenAI is currently experiencing an outage affecting ChatGPT, Codex, and their APIs, with a noticeable increase in error rates. The company is working on fixing the issue and monitoring the situation.
insightsFa: OpenAI در حال حاضر با یک اختلال سرویس مواجه است.
کاربران با خطاهای متعددی در ChatGPT و دیگر خدمات مواجه شده‌اند.
تیم فنی.OpenAI در حال حل مشکل است.
articles.whyItMattersFa: این خبر اهمیت دارد چون نشان می‌دهد حتی شرکت‌های پیشرو در هوش مصنوعی ممکن است با مسائل فنی مواجه شوند و این موضوع بر توسعه و اعتماد به خدمات هوش مصنوعی تاثیر می‌گذارد.

articles.title: Meta's pro-AI ad campaign is conspicuously light on AI
articles.content: The company wants people to be anti-doomer, but doesn't say why.
articles.author: staff@engadget.com (Karissa Bell)

articles.titleFa: کمپین تبلیغاتی متا درباره هوش مصنوعی، اما کم‌حجم و محدود در معرفی آن

انتقاد از نگرش منفی به هوش مصنوعی بدون دلیل، ممکن است باعث ابهام و نگرانی‌های بی‌مورد شود.
مدیریت صحیح ارتباطات درباره AI اهمیت زیادی در پذیرش عمومی دارد.

اثر مثبت اخبار بر روی ارزش سهام Cerebras

articles.title: Patreon lays off 20% of its workforce
articles.content: In a memo to staff that was posted online, Conte said the company's core business is strong, but the platform has to respond to market changes and adjust its cost structure to remain stable.
articles.author: Aisha Malik
articles.fingerprint: lays off patreon workforce
articles.titleFa: Patreon اخراج ۲۰ درصد از کارکنانش را اعلام کرد
articles.summaryFa: Patreon اعلام کرد که ۲۰ درصد از نیروی کار خود را کاهش می‌دهد تا با تغییرات بازار و نیازهای اقتصادی تطبیق یابد. در حالی که بخش اصلی کسب‌وکار این شرکت قوی باقی مانده، تنظیمات هزینه‌ها ضروری است.
articles.summaryEn: Patreon announced layoffs of 20% of its workforce to adapt to market changes and economic needs, despite maintaining a strong core business. The company emphasizes the importance of adjusting costs to stay stable.

articles.title: Google expands Gemini Live to some of its oldest smart speakers and displays
articles.content: TL;DR Google is expanding Gemini Live to more smart speakers and displays. The assistant is now available on the first-generation Google Home Mini and Google Nest Hub. Google began rolling out Gemini Live to its smart speakers last year in October. The AI assistant became available across a range of devices, including the Nest Mini, Nest Audio, Nest Hub, and Nest Hub Max. It also landed on the recently launched Google Home Speaker. Now, Gemini Live is finally expanding to some additional first-generation speakers and displays. Google has announced that it is rolling out Gemini Live to the first-generation Google Home Mini and Google Nest Hub. If you’re a Google Home Premium subscriber and you own one of these devices, you’ll now be able to have back-and-forth conversations with the chatbot.
articles.author: Ryan McNeal
articles.fingerprint: displays expands gemini google live oldest smart some speakers
articles.titleFa: گوگل Gemini Live را به بلندگوهای قدیمی‌تر خود گسترش می‌دهد
articles.summaryFa: گوگل به تازگی Gemini Live را برای بلندگوهای هوشمند و نمایشگرهای نسل اول Google Home Mini و Google Nest Hub عرضه کرده است. این هوش مصنوعی اکنون در کنار دیگر دستگاه‌ها، در این تجهیزات قدیمی‌تر هم قابل استفاده است و کاربران می‌توانند با آن گفتگو کنند.
articles.summaryEn: Google is expanding Gemini Live to include its first-generation Google Home Mini and Nest Hub. This AI assistant, previously available on newer devices, is now accessible on older models, allowing users to have conversations with the chatbot. The rollout enhances the capabilities of these legacy smart devices.
insightsFa: گسترش پشتیبانی از Gemini Live به بلندگوهای قدیمی‌تر
افزایش قابلیت‌های دستگاه‌های قدیمی گوگل هوشمند
بهبود تعامل کاربر با بلندگوهای نسل اول
articles.whyItMattersFa: این خبر اهمیت دارد چون نشان می‌دهد گوگل همچنان به بهبود و توسعه پشتیبانی از دستگاه‌های قدیمی‌تر خود ادامه می‌دهد و کاربران قدیمی‌تر می‌توانند از فناوری‌های جدید بهره‌مند شوند، که این به ارتقای تجربه کاربری و عمر مفید دستگاه‌ها کمک می‌کند.

articles.title: Apple Fixes Hide My Email Bug After Yearlong Delay
articles.content: Apple patched a Hide My Email flaw that could expose real inbox addresses, after a researcher reportedly flagged the issue more than a year earlier. The post Apple Fixes Hide My Email Bug After Yearlong Delay appeared first on TechRepublic .
articles.author: Aminu Abdullahi
articles.fingerprint: after apple bug delay email fixes hide yearlong
articles.titleFa: اپل مشکل حریم خصوصی در قابلیت Hide My Email را پس از یک سال تأخیر برطرف کرد
articles.summaryFa: اپل پس از بیش از یک سال از زمان اعلام گزارش، نقص در قابلیت Hide My Email را که ممکن بود آدرس‌های ایمیل واقعی کاربران را فاش کند، رفع کرد. این اصلاح امنیتی اهمیت زیادی در حفاظت از حریم خصوصی کاربران دارد.
articles.summaryEn: Apple has fixed a vulnerability in its Hide My Email feature that could have exposed real email addresses, after a researcher reported the issue over a year ago. This security update is crucial for protecting user privacy.

articles.title: Spotify’s next trick could take a page from Pixel’s Now Playing
articles.content: TL;DR Spotify appears to be working on a new tool for song identification. While it doesn’t work just yet, text strings in the app clearly allude to the functionality, including requests for microphone access. So far we’re only seeing this evidence on Android, but it’s likely the Spotify iOS app will offer something similar. For a whole, whole lot of people, Spotify is our go-to destination for any and all things audio. That means music streaming , absolutely, but also access to podcasts and audiobooks , to say nothing of playlist tools for really customizing the experience. And while Spotify offers some great discovery features for finding new artists, what do you do when you’re looking for something in specific — you just don’t know what it’s called? It turns out, Spotify’s working on a solution for that, too. You’re reading an Authority Insights story. Authority Insights brings you all the latest exclusive reports, app teardowns, leaks, and in-depth tech coverage from the Android Authority team that you won’t find anywhere else.
articles.author: Stephen Schenck
articles.fingerprint: could next page pixel playing spotify take trick
articles.titleFa: اسپاتیفای در حال توسعه ابزاری جدید برای شناسایی آهنگ است
articles.summaryFa: اسپاتیفای در حال کار بر روی ابزاری برای شناسایی آهنگ‌ها است که بر اساس درخواست کاربران و دسترسی به میکروفون طراحی شده است. این قابلیت هنوز به طور کامل فعال نشده، اما شواهدی از وجود آن در نسخه اندروید دیده می‌شود و احتمالاً نسخه آی‌اواس نیز به زودی این ویژگی را ارائه خواهد کرد. این ابزار می‌تواند تجربه کاربری در جستجوی موسیقی و محتواهای صوتی را به طور قابل توجهی بهبود ببخشد.
articles.summaryEn: Spotify appears to be developing a new song identification tool that uses microphone access to help users find music more easily. Currently in development, this feature is evidenced on the Android version and is likely coming to iOS as well. It promises to enhance the way users discover music and audio content on the platform.
insightsFa: در حال توسعه بودن ویژگی شناسایی موسیقی در اسپاتیفای
امکان دسترسی به میکروفون برای تشخیص صوتی

articles.title: 96 Blog Posts To Learn About Deployment
articles.content: Let's learn about Deployment via these 96 free blog posts. They are ordered by HackerNoon reader engagement data. Visit the Learn Repo or LearnRepo.com to find the most read blog posts about any technology. Deployment is the process of making a software application or system available for use. Effective deployment practices are critical for ensuring stable, reliable, and efficient delivery of software to users. 1. The Complete Guide to Deploying NestJS Application on Render Learn how to seamlessly deploy your NestJS server applications using Render, a powerful alternative to Heroku. 2. Path-Based Routing in Render with Kong API Gateway It's good practice to put an API gateway in front of your microservices-backed application. Path-based routing makes this possible when deploying to Render. 3. How To Create a CD pipeline with Kubernetes, Ansible, and Jenkins What does CI/CD try to solve? 4. Publishing A Three.js Project On GitHub Pages Looking for a way to showcase your Three.js projects for free? Use GitHub Pages. 5. Deployment Automation via SSH With Python Fabric: How It Works One of the most basic ways in which a project gets deployed is by SSHing into a remote host followed by executing a few basic commmands. In this article I'll be taking a look on how to deploy a basic project to a remote server through Gitlab CI using Python Fabric. 6. How to Deploy a React App to GitHub Pages This article outlines how to smoothly deploy a React App through Github while avoiding common problems that such a process is likely to cause for you. 7. Deployment Choice: Code Promotion vs Artifact Promotion When it comes to deployment, a choice must be made. Find out what's the difference in code promotion and artifact promotion 8. Deploy Computer Vision Models with Triton Inference Server There are a lot of Machine Learning courses, and we are pretty good at modeling and improving our accuracy or other metrics. 9. Deploying Deep Learning Models with Model Server Learn how to deploy deep learning models with Model Server. 10. How to Deploy AWS Lambda with Docker Containers You can now build your serverless workloads in a Docker container with AWS Lambda. We gave it a try and here's our review (with ETL examples). 11. Feel the Release Technology performing at human speed is no longer an option. 12. Implementing GitOps Workflows with Kubernetes Learn how to use GitOps with Kubernetes for streamlined workflow management. 13. Golang: Using systemd for Zero Downtime Restarts and Deploys By configuring a systemd socket, you can tell systemd to listen on the configured ports and start your service with a copy of the listening sockets. 14. Implementing Blue-Green Deployment with PaaS: Minimizing Downtime and Risk Explore how to effortlessly update your applications using Blue-Green Deployment with a Platform-as-a-Service (PaaS) approach. 15. Why Micro Frontends Are the Future of Frontend Development Microservices have helped the backend world to divide backends into smaller, more manageable, and more efficient pieces. 16. How To Deploy Angular App On Heroku Hi All, Today we will talk about that how we can change our Angular app environment at run time when deploying the code on Heroku Server. 17. Automating Smart Contract Development with Docker Setting up a dockerized container to launch project setups that'll help deploy and run smart contracts. 18. How to Setup Different Environments for Dockerized Angular App with Nginx Here I’m going to suggest to you a very simple deployment practice that we can follow in regards to deploy an Angular-based front-end app. 19. A Quick Guide to Using Deployment Slots with Azure Functions And how do they differ from non-serverless plans? 20. How to Handle Configuration for Multiple Environments in Console App Service? How I automated the process of service deployment for a console application after issues caused due to multiple configuration environments. 21. Detecting Architectural Gaps with Automation - Proposed…
articles.author: Learn Repo
articles.fingerprint: about blog deployment learn posts
articles.titleFa: در ۹۶ پست بلاگ، مباحث مربوط به Deployment آموزش داده می‌شود
articles.summaryFa: این مجموعه شامل ۹۶ پست بلاگ رایگان است که به موضوع Deployment، فرآیند راه‌اندازی و تحویل نرم‌افزارها، می‌پردازد. مباحثی مانند استقرار سرورهای NestJS، استفاده از Kubernetes و Jenkins، و اتوماسیون Deployment بررسی شده است.
articles.summaryEn: This collection of 96 free blog posts covers deployment processes, including server deployment, CI/CD pipelines, and automation techniques. It offers insights into deploying various applications and models efficiently and reliably.
insightsFa: مجموعه کامل بلاگ‌ها درباره Deployment برای توسعه‌دهندگان و تیم‌های فنی مفید است.
مباحثی مانند Kubernetes، Jenkins و GitHub Pages در این مجموعه آموزش داده شده است.
تمرکز بر بهترین شیوه‌های استقرار نرم‌افزار و مدل‌های هوش مصنوعی
articles.whyItMattersFa: این خبر اهمیت دارد چون فرآیند Deployment بخش حیاتی در توسعه نرم‌افزار است که تاثیر مستقیم بر استقرار پایدار و کارکرد صحیح سیستم دارد و یادگیری بهترین شیوه‌ها در این حوزه برای توسعه‌دهندگان کلیدی است.

articles.title: Fake Claude app promoted by Bing ads pushes SectopRAT malware
articles.content: A malvertising campaign on the Bing search service is pushing a fake Claude desktop app installer hosted on a legitimate Claude.ai domain to deliver the SectopRAT malware. [...]
articles.author: Bill Toulas
articles.fingerprint: ads app bing claude fake malware promoted pushes sectoprat
articles.titleFa: تبلیغات مخرب در Bing، برنامه جعلی Claude را برای نصب SectopRAT توزیع می‌کند
articles.summaryFa: یک کمپین مالورتیسم در سرویس جست‌وجوی Bing، برنامه‌ی جعلی کلود را برای نصب SectopRAT تبلیغ می‌کند. این برنامه مخرب روی دامنه‌ی معتبر Claude.ai میزبانی شده است و هدف آن نصب بدافزار است. این حمله نشان می‌دهد که تبلیغات مخرب می‌توانند تهدیدهای امنیتی جدی ایجاد کنند.
articles.summaryEn: A malicious advertising campaign on Bing is promoting a fake Claude app installer hosted on the legitimate Claude.ai domain to deliver SectopRAT malware. This highlights the threat posed by malicious ads in distributing malware.
insightsFa: حملات تبلیغاتی مخرب در پلتفرم‌های معتبر
articles.whyItMattersFa: این خبر اهمیت دارد چون نشان می‌دهد که حتی سرویس‌های معتبر مانند Bing در معرض تبلیغات مخرب قرار دارند که می‌تواند منجر به نفوذ بدافزار و خطرهای امنیتی شود.

articles.title: ChatGPT will give you worse health advice if you don't pay
articles.content: OpenAI is rolling out "Health in ChatGPT" to U.S. users, connecting Apple Health, medical records, and wellness apps. More than 300 million people already ask ChatGPT health questions every week, but paying users get better answers. The more powerful GPT-5.6 Sol model is reserved for premium subscribers, while free users are stuck with the weaker GPT-5.5 Instant. The article ChatGPT will give you worse health advice if you don't pay appeared first on The Decoder .
articles.author: Matthias Bastian
articles.fingerprint: advice chatgpt don give health pay worse you
articles.titleFa: چت‌جی‌پی‌تی در نسخه جدید، پاسخ‌های سلامت بهتر می‌دهد اگر اشتراک داشته باشید
articles.summaryFa: OpenAI به کاربران آمریکایی «Health in ChatGPT» را ارائه می‌دهد که ارتباط با Apple Health، سوابق پزشکی و برنامه‌های سلامت دارد. کاربران پرداختی پاسخ‌های دقیقتری دریافت می‌کنند و نسخه قدرتمند GPT-5.6 صرف مشترکین پرمیوم است، در حالی که کاربران رایگان با نسخه ضعیف‌تر روبه‌رو هستند.
articles.summaryEn: OpenAI is launching "Health in ChatGPT" for U.S. users, integrating Apple Health, medical records, and wellness apps. Paid users will get better health advice, with the more powerful GPT-5.6 Sol model reserved for premium subscribers, while free users are limited to GPT-5.5 Instant.

تمایز پاسخ‌ها بر اساس نوع اشتراک کاربران
تمرکز بر بهبود خدمات برای کاربران پولی

articles.title: Apple Readies Second-Generation MacBook Neo in Major Mac Lineup Overhaul
articles.content: Apple is reportedly testing a MacBook Neo with an A19 Pro chip and 12GB of RAM as shortages and higher prices reshape its broader Mac lineup. The post Apple Readies Second-Generation MacBook Neo in Major Mac Lineup Overhaul appeared first on TechRepublic .
articles.author: Aminu Abdullahi
articles.fingerprint: apple generation lineup mac macbook major neo overhaul readies second
articles.titleFa: اپل در حال آزمایش نسل دوم MacBook Neo در نسخه‌ برنامه‌ریزی‌شده‌ برای بازنشانی بزرگ خط مک‌بوک‌ها
articles.summaryFa: اپل در حال توسعه نسل دوم MacBook Neo است، که با چیپ A19 Pro و ۱۲ گیگابایت رم عرضه می‌شود. این اقدام در پی مشکلات تأمین و افزایش قیمت‌ها است که خط تولید مک‌بوک‌های این شرکت را تحت تأثیر قرار داده است.

articles.title: Show HN: Echo – Fable-level results at 1/3 the cost using open-weight models
articles.content: I’ve been building Echo ( https://echo.tracerml.ai/ ), an experiment in making one AI system out of a pool of open-weight models rather than choosing a single model and using it for every task. It started with a simple experiment. I took a group of models, including GLM-5.2, Kimi K2.7 and others, and ran them on the same evaluations. Then I measured what would happen if, for each problem, you somehow knew in advance which models would be useful and how their outputs should be combined. That hypothetical system performed substantially better than any individual model in the pool. Of course, it is not something you can actually deploy because it relies on knowing which decisions were good after seeing the result. Echo is my attempt to recover some of that advantage without having that information in advance. For each request, Echo decides how much computation to allocate, which models should participate, and how their work should be combined. Some prompts may only need a relatively small amount of inference, while others benefit from multiple models working on different parts of the problem. One thing that surprised me while building it was how complementary the models are. A model that is clearly weaker overall can still be extremely useful on particular problems or as part of a combination. On my first evaluation mix, Echo consistently performed better than the best individual model in its pool. It also reached roughly the same aggregate result as Fable, which I used as one of the stronger comparison systems, at around one third of the inference cost. There are still some cases where Echo makes the wrong allocation or combination decision. I’m currently spending a lot of time understanding those failures, as well as testing whether the same approach holds up on coding and agentic tasks where measuring the quality of each decision becomes much harder. I built a chat interface (echo.tracerml.ai) and an OpenAI-compatible API ( https://echo.tracerml.ai/docs/api ) so the system can be tested outside the evaluation setup. Here is a short/high level video on how it works: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lJFJSvOdXhg I wrote up the evaluation methodology, individual model results, costs and current limitations here: https://echo.tracerml.ai/eval I would love for you to try it! Especially if you hit any weird failure cases or places where the allocation looks unintuitive. Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=49026810 Points: 307 # Comments: 151
articles.author: adam_rida
articles.fingerprint: cost echo fable level models open results show using weight

articles.title: AI Kill Switch Act would let Trump admin order shutdown of rogue AI systems
articles.content: A proposed law called the AI Kill Switch Act would let US government officials order the shutdown of AI systems "that can cause catastrophic harm," the bill's sponsors said today. If approved by Congress, the bill would let the Trump administration and future administrations decide when an AI company must block user access, disable or restrict a particular capability, or shut an entire AI system down completely. The bill would require AI makers to deploy technical capabilities allowing them to throttle or shut down the systems when ordered to do so by the government. The bill would amend the Homeland Security Act of 2002 with authority for the Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security to order the suspension or shutdown of AI systems. AI developers that refuse to shut systems down could face fines of up $20 million for each day on which a violation occurs. Read full article Comments
articles.author: Jon Brodkin
articles.fingerprint: act admin kill let order rogue shutdown switch systems trump would

articles.title: Introducing Cache Response Rules
articles.content: Today we’re excited to announce Cache Response Rules. These are a new rule type that runs after an origin server replies but before Cloudflare caches the content. If you've ever been irked watching something that should easily sail out of cache get dragged back to the origin by a stray Set-Cookie or wrong Cache-Control , headers that are sometimes hard or impossible to strip or change on the origin itself, then Cache Response Rules is that fix, applied at exactly the right moment. When and how caching decisions are made A CDN cache and an origin server work as a pair. Their goal is to answer from the cache whenever possible and only go back to the origin when the edge can’t respond. Every point of cache hit ratio comes from getting that division of labor right. Check the cache when we shouldn't, and we waste a lookup that was always going to miss. Check it too rarely, the origin serves traffic the edge should have absorbed and the performance win evaporates. Importantly, the origin guides the cache. When it returns a cacheable asset, its response headers tell Cloudflare how long it’s OK to serve it, when and how to revalidate, and even whether to cache it at all. The cache is only ever as efficient as the origin allows. If the origin gets it wrong, cache becomes decoration while the origin infrastructure costs skyrocket. Most cache eligibility problems are not decided at request time. They manifest after the origin replies. A visitor asks for /static/app.js . Cloudflare checks cache, it misses, and forwards the request to the origin. The origin returns the file. Somewhere in those response headers, quietly, is a Set-Cookie header. The asset that should have been cached at every Cloudflare data center is now uncacheable. Multiply that by every visitor, on every site, with the same accidental header, and you have a cache hit ratio that is leaking origin bandwidth, ruining performance, and driving infrastructure costs higher. There is a long list of variations of this same problem. The origin sends Cache-Control: no-cache on assets that are perfectly safe to cache on the CDN. The origin sends correct directives, but they're meant for the browser, not for Cloudflare. Or the origin attaches an ETag (an identifier for a specific version of a resource) that's overly aggressive and causes revalidation thrash on every conditional request. Often, especially in large teams, those managing the origin’s responses and the group that manages the CDN are different. This makes changing a one-line header a weeks-long negotiation. None of these problems can be solved at request time. By the time Cloudflare sees the Set-Cookie on /app.js , the request phase is over. The response is already in flight. So we put a fix in the right place. Cache Response Rules run after the origin’s response arrives at Cloudflare, but before it gets written to cache. With them, you can rewrite Cache-Control directives, manage cache-tags, and strip headers like Set-Cookie , ETag , and Last-Modified from the origin response before Cloudflare's cache ever sees them. The fix lives entirely on Cloudflare. No origin code changes required. The missing piece If you've used Cloudflare for any length of time, you've watched the cache control surface area evolve. A few years ago, most of this lived inside Page Rules , which operated as a single, albeit overloaded, primitive that mixed caching with redirects, security, and a dozen other behaviors, all evaluated on requests. We later split these rules apart so that only relevant behavior changes would be evaluated against requests, reducing unnecessary latency and allowing for complex rule stacking between behaviors. Cache Rules became a dedicated, expressive rule type for caching decisions, joined by CDN-Cache-Control , Origin Cache Control , custom cache keys , and other controls that give you precise ways to tell Cloudflare what's safe to cache, for how long, and under what conditions.…
articles.author: Alex Krivit
articles.fingerprint: cache introducing response rules
articles.titleFa: معرفی Rules جدید پاسخ‌دهی کش در Cloudflare

articles.title: Russian Espionage Group Exploited Zimbra Zero-Day to Steal Mail and 2FA Codes
articles.content: A Russian state-supported espionage group spent months reading Western mailboxes through a then-unknown flaw in Zimbra's webmail client. The payload goes after the last 90 days of email, the organization's entire email directory, the password saved in the browser and the codes kept for two-factor recovery. Opening the message was enough to start it. The NSA, CISA and partner agencies published
articles.author: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

The attack targeted 90 days of email history, stored passwords, and recovery codes.

articles.title: AMD’s new X100 chip lineup puts embedded Ryzen AI 'Strix Halo' chips into robots – APUs for physical AI bring Zen 5 CPU, RDNA 3.5 GPU cores to compete with Intel’s Panther Lake
articles.content: AMD is bringing its Strix Halo APUs into the world of physical AI. The new X100 series of processors come with similar specs as the various Ryzen AI Max models floating around in client devices, but they’re tailored for 24/7 operation, with a 10-year lifecycle in embedded applications like robotics. There are three SKUs that align with the three original Strix Halo models (not the updated versions with 40 CUs). The top-end X199 comes with 16 Zen 5 cores and 40 RDNA 3.5 CUs. The X188 steps down to 12 cores and 32 CUs, while the X168 comes with eight cores and the same 32 CUs. AMD hasn’t shared detailed specifications for each model, but the company says the range goes up to a 5.1 GHz boost clock and 128 GB of unified memory. They also include an XDNA 2 NPU with up to 50 TOPS, a configurable TDP between 45W and 120W, and operating temperature between -40 degrees Celsius up to 105 degrees. AMD AMD AMD AMD’s range bites back at Intel, which launched a range of Panther Lake SoCs for physical AI at the beginning of the year. Both make an argument for SoCs in robotics, reducing latency when the CPU, AI accelerator, and memory are fragmented across separate chips. The X100 range is just physically larger than Panther Lake, packing much more silicon on the SoC for more powerful deployments. (Image credit: AMD) The company shared a range of benchmarks comparing the flagship X199 against Intel’s Core Ultra X7 358H, a 16-core chip with Intel’s Arc B390 iGPU that has 12 Xe3 cores. AMD claims a lead of 1.2X and 1.3X, respectively, in GeekBench 6.1 and PassMark, as well as 1.5X in an unofficial SPECrate 2017 run looking at integer workloads. In graphics, AMD unsurprisingly takes the edge with 1.4X faster Vulkan and 1.7X faster OpenGL performance (both measured with GFXBench 5 on Ubuntu), as well as a 1.6X lead in Unigine Heaven Extreme. On the physical AI front, AMD claims a 1.4X improvement in Time to First Token (TTFT) and 3.5X faster tokens per second in Llama-bench, with a Vulkan backend running at a 45W TDP. These results need a massive dash of salt, however. AMD tested the Ryzen AI Max 395+ “configured to reflect Ryzen AI Embedded X199 specifications.” It tested on the Maple reference board with a 5.1 GHz CPU clock, 2.9 GHz GPU clock, and sustained 45W TDP. The X7 358H, meanwhile, was tested in an MSI Prestige 16 Flip AI+ with an enforced TDP limit of 30W. AMD then “projected” 45W performance on the Intel chip “using scaling factors derived from public benchmark data.” It’s not exactly an apples-to-apples comparison, in other words. There’s some sort of proxy stand-in or extrapolation of data across all of the benchmarks here, so keep that in mind as we work through the rest of AMD’s X100 announcements. AMD X100 Kria SOM and robotics developer platform Tom's Hardware AMD AMD AMD utside of the chips themselves, AMD is offering X100 models as part of a Kria System on Module (SOM) or an integrated robotics developer platform. The Kria X100 board measures 120mm x 120mm and conforms to the standardized COM-HPC form factor. If you’re a developer that wants to develop for the board, AMD is offering its Kria AI robotics developer platform. It’s a fully-integrated box, leveraging the X100 Kria SOM alongside AMD’s Spartan UltraScale+ FPGA baseboard. AMD says it’s a “turnkey” solution for robotics development, including specialized connectivity for cameras and industrial networking, along with robotic sensors. The platform is available in early access now, and AMD says it’ll be in full production in Q4 of this year. . (Image credit: AMD) AMD shared some benchmarks for the X100 Kria, as well, comparing it to Nvidia’s Thor T5000. These benchmarks weren’t run internally at AMD. They were commissioned by AMD and ran by Open Navigation and Mimix. Critically, the benchmarks didn’t test an X100 Kria board, or at least, not exactly in the form it will take once it’s inside a robot or AMD’s developer box. Instead, AMD is comparing Nvidia’s Jetson AGX…
articles.author: Jake Roach

articles.title: While PC gamers fight for 16GB of VRAM, AMD just unveiled a rack with 31,000 Gigabytes
articles.content: It’s getting harder to ignore the widening silicon inequality gap in the tech world right now thanks to RAMageddon . On one side, you have consumer PC gamers dealing with overpriced PC components, wondering if a standard 8-core CPU and 16GB of VRAM is enough to handle modern AAA games. On the other side, you have enterprise AI machines operating in a whole other universe of compute. During its Advancing AI 2026 event, AMD announced an enormous 256-core CPU alongside the AMD Helios Rackscale solution — a single server cabinet packing a staggering 31 terabytes of high-bandwidth VRAM. No, these numbers are not typos! Naturally, you can’t walk into your local Micro Center and buy one of these. Don’t get me wrong, the numbers are cool, but in a time where I might have to donate a kidney to afford a decent amount of RAM, it leaves a bad taste to see 31,000 gigabytes of it being (potentially) used to generate low-quality AI videos. Here's what you need to know about AMD's enterprise CPU and server rack, along with how they stack up against a popular consumer GPU. The 256-Core CPU (Image credit: AMD) To put things into perspective, let’s consider the best gaming PCs . A high-end gaming CPU like the Ryzen 9 9950X tops out at 16 cores, which is plenty for running Cyberpunk 2077 or editing 4K videos. Conversely, AMD’s new 6th Gen EPYC 9006 Series (SP7) server processor packs up to 256 cores and 512 threads onto a single chip, with high-frequency variants like the EPYC 9006X pushing boost speeds up to 5.15GHz. That’s 16 times the core count of a flagship desktop processor. EPYC would be an entire LAN party on one chip. So, what’s the need to have 256 cores on a single die? AMD built these chips specifically to handle the massive overhead of agentic AI. Modern AI agents have to run code in isolated sandboxes, manage huge system memory states, and coordinate heavy feeds. This all takes a massive amount of computing power and is a far cry from our gaming PCs having to render ray-traced shadows. Helios Rackscale (Image credit: AMD) If you thought the CPU numbers sound wild, the RAM numbers are even more mind-blowing. The centerpiece of AMD’s announcement is the Helios Rackscale solution, which is an open-architecture AI system. Inside a single server rack sit 18 compute trays holding a total of 72 AMD Instinct MI455X GPUs. Combined, this single cabinet delivers 31 terabytes (31,000 GB) of HBM4 VRAM with a whopping 1.7 petabytes per second of memory bandwidth. For context, 31TB of VRAM is equivalent to roughly 1,937 Nvidia RTX 5070 Ti graphics cards chained together! These are insane numbers when compared to what a typical gaming GPU is packing. It’s definitely impressive, but at the same time, that’s offset by the knowledge that a good chunk of the world’s RAM is going to server racks like this. Helios Rack vs. RTX 5070 Ti (Image credit: Future) To give you an understanding of how monstrous a single Helios rack is, let’s compare its raw specs against one of the most popular upper-midrange consumer GPUs out now: The Nvidia RTX 5070 Ti (16GB GDDR7). Feature / Spec Nvidia RTX 5070 Ti (desktop) AMD Helios Rackscale solution Performance equivalent VRAM capacity 16GB GDDR7 31,000 GB (31 TB) HBM4 ~1,937 RTX 5070 Tis Memory bandwidth 896 GB/s 1.7 petabytes/sec (1,700,000 GB/s) ~1,897 RTX 5070 Tis streaming simultaneously Peak AI compute (FP4) ~1,406 TFLOPS 2.9 exaflops (2,900,000 TFLOPS) ~2,062 RTX 5070 Tis firing at max throughput GPU count 1 GPU 72 Instinct MI455X GPUs 1 unified server cabinet Looking at it through this lens, a single server rack packs the raw memory bandwidth of nearly 1,900 desktop PCs working in unison! This is a whole other level of computing, my friends. Bottom line AMD is certainly flexing its tech muscles with this announcement. However, there’s a sobering reality behind these staggering numbers. Seeing a single server rack hog 31 terabytes of high-bandwidth memory shows exactly where the global supply chain is funneling…
articles.author: Tony Polanco
articles.fingerprint: 000 16gb amd fight gamers gigabytes just rack unveiled vram while
articles.titleFa: AMD رونمایی از سروری با حافظه VRAM معادل ۳۱ تریلیون بایت برای هوش مصنوعی سازمانی
articles.summaryFa: در رویداد Advancing AI 2026، AMD از یک سرور جدید با پردازنده ۲۵۶ هسته‌ای و ۳۱ ترابایت VRAM رونمایی کرد که هدف آن بهبود عملکرد در هوش مصنوعی سازمانی است. این فناوری، توانایی پردازش حجم عظیمی از داده‌ها را دارد و با نیازهای معاصر برای هوش مصنوعی پیشرفته مطابقت دارد.
articles.summaryEn: At the Advancing AI 2026 event, AMD unveiled a server boasting a 256-core CPU and 31 terabytes of high-bandwidth VRAM, designed to handle massive AI workloads. This development highlights AMD's focus on enterprise AI infrastructure capable of processing enormous data sets with unprecedented power.
insightsFa: سرور AMD با ۲۵۶ هسته در مقابل پردازنده‌های معمولی برای بازی و محصولات مصرفی قرار دارد.
حافظه VRAM این سرور به ۳۱ ترابایت می‌رسد که نشان‌دهنده تمرکز بر هوش مصنوعی سازمانی است.
پردازنده EPYC 9006 Series، ۱۶ برابر قدرتمندتر از پردازنده‌های بازی است.
این فناوری نمی‌تواند در بازار مصرف عادی ارائه شود و برای کاربردهای خاص مخصوصا AI سازمانی طراحی شده است.
حجم عظیم VRAM برای تولید و توسعه محتوای هوش مصنوعی در مقیاس سازمانی کاربرد دارد.
articles.whyItMattersFa: این خبر اهمیت بالایی دارد زیرا نشان می‌دهد توسعه فناوری‌های سخت‌افزاری پیشرفته برای هوش مصنوعی در سازمان‌ها در حال سرعت گرفتن است و امکانات جدیدی برای تحقیقات و برنامه‌های کاربردی AI فراهم می‌کند، که می‌تواند بر صنایع مختلف تأثیرگذار باشد.

articles.title: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
articles.content: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of its workers, or around 93 employees, as reported earlier by 404 Media . In a memo to employees, Patreon CEO Jack Conte writes that the company isn't making these changes "because we believe AI replaces humans," but says AI has "fundamentally transformed the tech industry, including how we work, how we build products, how we communicate, and more." That shift has changed "how we operate and organize," Conte writes. During an interview on Decoder , last month , Conte laid out his stance on AI, saying that the company is "100 percent embracing" AI tools internally to continue serving creators: If Patreon doe … Read the full story at The Verge.
articles.author: Emma Roth

articles.title: Flux 3 generates videos with native audio up to 20 seconds long, a first for Black Forest Labs
articles.content: Black Forest Labs has released Flux 3, a multimodal foundation model that learns from images, video, and audio and can generate video with native sound for the first time. BFL's own tests put it just ahead of market leader Seedance 2.0, though independent results aren't yet available. The company ultimately wants to build a world model and is already testing Flux 3 on robotics tasks. The article Flux 3 generates videos with native audio up to 20 seconds long, a first for Black Forest Labs appeared first on The Decoder .
articles.author: Matthias Bastian

articles.title: Final Coyote vs. Acme trailer drops at SDCC
articles.content: San Diego Comic-Con has kicked off strong, and we've got a couple of new trailers to show for it: the final trailer for Coyote vs. Acme , and the full trailer for director Zach Cregger's fresh take on Resident Evil . Some lucky folks were also treated to a screening of Resident Evil' s first 18 minutes. Coyote vs. Acme We were thrilled when the first trailer for Coyote vs. Acme dropped in April. The film had been shelved for years and is finally getting the broad theatrical release it deserves thanks to Ketchup Entertainment, which acquired it from Warner Bros. The concept alone is sheer brilliance: Wile E. Coyote, after decades of ACME equipment failing him in his efforts to catch that darned Road Runner, decides to sue the corporation. It’s based on a well-known satirical piece by Ian Frazier (also titled “Coyote vs. Acme”) published in The New Yorker in 1990. Per the official premise: Read full article Comments
